audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + en + clause]]. Use [[grammar:en]] to join two complete ideas.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces coordinating [[grammar:en]] in [[grammar:clause + en + clause]], linking two finite main clauses while retaining overt subjects in both conjuncts.

audienceSections.normal:
#### idea + en + idea

Use [[grammar:en]] to join two complete ideas, as in [[grammar:Mark werkt en Mark drinkt koffie.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + en + clause

The pattern [[grammar:clause + en + clause]] uses [[grammar:en]] to coordinate two independent clauses, as in [[grammar:Mark werkt en Mark drinkt koffie.]] Each conjunct retains an overt subject and finite predicate; ellipsis and other coordinators are outside this chapter.

audienceSections.normal: The words [[grammar:eerst]], [[grammar:ochtend]], [[grammar:middag]], and [[grammar:avond]] help you follow the day's order.
audienceSections.expert: Temporal framing and sequence adverb [[grammar:eerst]] organize the discourse, while repeated subject [[grammar:Mark]] keeps conjunct structure transparent.
The coordination and routine predicates are standard across major Dutch varieties.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Het is ochtend.]] starts the day.
- [[grammar:Mark gaat eerst naar het kantoor.]] names his first destination.
- [[grammar:Mark werkt en Mark drinkt koffie.]] joins two actions with [[grammar:en]].
- [[grammar:Mark leest de krant in de pauze.]] says what he does during the break.
- [[grammar:Het is middag.]] moves the story to the afternoon.
- [[grammar:Mark gaat naar de markt en Mark koopt brood.]] joins going and buying.
- [[grammar:Mark koopt fruit en Mark gaat naar het kantoor.]] joins another two actions.
- [[grammar:Het is avond.]] ends the day.
breakdown.expert:
- [[grammar:Het is ochtend.]], [[grammar:Het is middag.]], and [[grammar:Het is avond.]] are parallel temporal setting frames.
- [[grammar:Mark gaat eerst naar het kantoor.]] combines a finite motion predicate, sequence adverb, and destination phrase.
- [[grammar:Mark werkt en Mark drinkt koffie.]] coordinates two overt-subject finite clauses.
- [[grammar:Mark leest de krant in de pauze.]] places a temporal prepositional phrase after the object.
- [[grammar:Mark gaat naar de markt en Mark koopt brood.]] coordinates motion and transitive acquisition predicates.
- [[grammar:Mark koopt fruit en Mark gaat naar het kantoor.]] reverses that discourse order while preserving full clauses.
